pattern: Try every split of k between the two arrays; for each split extract the max subsequence from each using a greedy stack, then merge the two subsequences by always picking the lexicographically larger prefix.
hints: For any fixed split (i digits from nums1, k-i from nums2), the problem decomposes into independently finding the maximum subsequence of length i from nums1 and maximum of length k-i from nums2, then merging them.

To extract the maximum subsequence of length t from an array of length m, use a monotone decreasing stack with a budget of m-t discards: pop the top when it is smaller than the current digit and discards remain.
When merging two already-maximised sequences, always take from the sequence whose remaining suffix is lexicographically larger — compare element by element to break ties correctly.

edge_inputs.description: classic example mixing two arrays of different lengths
k equals total length — take all digits
repeated leading digits require careful merge tie-breaking
k equals 1 — just pick the global max digit
all zeros — result is all zeros
one array contributes nothing to the best split
equal digits throughout — merge must still be deterministic
k equals 1 from a pair of single-element arrays

stress.description: medium-length arrays with digit values to stress merge and extraction
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = size
    n = size
    nums1 = [rng.randint(0, 9) for _ in range(m)]
    nums2 = [rng.randint(0, 9) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, m + n)
    return [nums1, nums2, k]
